name: RevenueCat API
what_it_does: Manages in-app purchases and subscription lifecycle across iOS, Android, and web, handling receipt validation, entitlement tracking, and paywall delivery through a unified API.
use_cases: Validate and track iOS and Android in-app purchase receipts without building platform-specific server-side logic
Gate app features using entitlements that automatically update when subscriptions lapse or upgrade
Query a user's current subscription status from a backend agent to personalize content or feature access
Analyze subscription cohorts, churn rates, and MRR trends via RevenueCat's dashboard and data exports
Deliver dynamic paywalls with A/B testing using RevenueCat Paywalls without deploying new app builds
not_for: Web-only SaaS products that use Stripe or Paddle for subscriptions and have no mobile app component
High-volume B2B billing with complex invoice, contract, or metered usage requirements
best_when: Best when building mobile apps on iOS and/or Android that need cross-platform subscription parity, reliable receipt validation, and subscription analytics without maintaining your own purchase server.
avoid_when: Avoid when your product is web-only or when you need complex metered billing, invoicing, or enterprise contract management beyond simple subscription tiers.

auth.notes: Public SDK key used in client apps; secret API key (bearer token) used for server-side REST calls. Separate keys per app per platform. App User ID ties purchases to users.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free up to $2,500 tracked MRR with full feature access
pricing.paid_tiers: 1% of tracked MRR plus $0.01 per transaction above $2,500 MRR; volume discounts for high-MRR apps
pricing.notes: Revenue-share pricing scales naturally with app success. No upfront monthly fee until $2.5k MRR is reached.

performance.rate_limits: REST API: 150 requests/minute per secret key; SDK calls are batched internally

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with code and message fields. SDK errors are platform-idiomatic (NSError on iOS, Exception on Android).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET endpoints are fully idempotent. POST endpoints for granting entitlements are idempotent by app_user_id but should not be called in retry loops without checking current state first.
known_agent_gotchas: Subscription status can have a brief propagation delay (seconds to minutes) after a purchase event before the REST API reflects the new state
App User ID aliasing (when anonymous IDs merge with identified users) can create duplicate subscriber records if not handled carefully
Webhook payloads require HMAC signature verification — missing this step allows spoofed events in automated workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: Clear separation between public SDK key and secret REST key. Webhook HMAC verification documented and recommended. SOC2 Type II certified.
